description: SMS copy evaluation rubric. 8 dimensions tuned for ultra-short (≤160 char) one-shot messages. Many email/meta dimensions don't apply — there's no subject line, no body flow, no scroll. The score rewards: clear single purpose, fits one segment, link/CTA present when needed, opt-out present for marketing sends, and brand voice intact in tiny space.

dimensions.name: Angle Clarity
dimensions.description: Is the single job of the SMS unmistakable in one read? SMS has no scroll — clarity is everything.

scoring_guide.1: Cryptic. Recipient has to re-read or guess what the message is for.
scoring_guide.2: Multiple themes packed in. Reader has to parse which thing matters.
scoring_guide.3: Job is identifiable but blurred by extra detail.
scoring_guide.4: Clear single job. Reader gets it on first read.
scoring_guide.5: Unmistakable in one read. Every word reinforces the one purpose.

dimensions.name: Motivation Match
dimensions.description: Does the SMS hit what the recipient cares about right now (e.g. round opens = act, confirmation = relief)?

scoring_guide.1: Wrong motivation entirely. Tone or job mismatched to audience state.
scoring_guide.2: Generic. Could be sent to anyone — no recognition of recipient context.
scoring_guide.4: Lands the felt need (urgency, reassurance, etc.) appropriately for the audience segment.
scoring_guide.5: Reads like it was written for this recipient at this exact moment.

dimensions.name: Specificity
dimensions.description: Concrete brand name, link, or named action — not vague filler.

scoring_guide.1: Entirely vague. No brand, no link, no concrete handle.
scoring_guide.2: One concrete element (brand or link) but the rest is filler.
scoring_guide.3: Brand named + one other concrete element.
scoring_guide.4: Brand + link + clear named action all present.
scoring_guide.5: Every word earns its place — brand, action, link, opt-out, all surgical.

dimensions.name: Character Efficiency
dimensions.description: Fits in one SMS segment (≤160 chars including merge-tag expansion). Two-segment max acceptable for confirmations.

scoring_guide.2: Two segments (161-320) when single was achievable.
scoring_guide.3: Two segments and content justifies it (e.g. confirmation with email contact).
scoring_guide.4: Single segment but uses 140-160 chars — could be tighter.
scoring_guide.5: Single segment, ≤140 chars. Maximum punch per character.

deterministic_rules.two_segment_max: ≤320 chars for confirmations or compliance-required content

dimensions.name: CTA Clarity
dimensions.description: Is the next action obvious — tap the link, reply, or no action needed (confirmation)?

scoring_guide.1: Action ambiguous. Recipient unsure what to do.
scoring_guide.4: Clear single action — tap link, reply, or 'no action needed' explicit.
scoring_guide.5: Action is obvious and frictionless — link is the natural focal point or 'you're in' confirms no action.

dimensions.name: Compliance Fit
dimensions.description: Marketing sends include opt-out (Reply STOP). Investment/CSF sends route via link to risk-warning page (the regulatory workaround for SMS char limits). Transactional confirmations need neither.

scoring_guide.1: Marketing send missing opt-out, OR investment-context SMS with no link to risk warning.
scoring_guide.2: Opt-out attempted but unclear; or risk-warning link buried.
scoring_guide.3: Opt-out present for marketing, OR link to risk-warning context for investment SMS.
scoring_guide.4: Both compliance signals correct for context (marketing = opt-out; investment = link).
scoring_guide.5: Compliance is woven naturally — the link IS the offer destination, the opt-out IS the final phrase, no awkward bolt-ons.

deterministic_rules.marketing_opt_out: Look for 'Reply STOP' or 'STOP to opt out' (only required for marketing audience, not transactional confirmations)
deterministic_rules.investment_link_workaround: Investment-context SMS should contain a URL — that URL is the safe-harbour route to the risk-warning page

dimensions.name: Tone & Brand Fit
dimensions.description: Does the SMS sound like the brand in 160 chars — not robotic, not corporate?

scoring_guide.1: Generic platform-template tone. No brand voice.
scoring_guide.4: Brand voice intact — warm/founder/factual as appropriate to segment.
scoring_guide.5: Sounds unmistakably like the brand — even at 160 chars the voice is preserved.

dimensions.name: Differentiation
dimensions.description: Distinct from other SMS in the set (e.g. confirmation vs round-opens vs reminder all sound different).

scoring_guide.1: Indistinguishable from another SMS in set.
scoring_guide.2: Surface differences (URL only) but core message overlaps.
scoring_guide.5: Unique entry point — recipient seeing both would know exactly which is which.
